text.title: Mistake Prevention Layers
text.summary: Four-layer defense against recurring mistakes: pre-task warnings, quality gates, CI automation, and multi-model review.

section.title: Layer 1: Pre-Task Warning Injection

rule.statement: Before executing any task, keyword-search mistakes.md and prepend matching entries as a Warnings section in the task prompt.
rule.rationale: Agents that skip pre-task loading get no benefit from the accumulated mistake corpus. Injection must be explicit and automated, not optional.

example.code: # Load warnings before executing TASK-042 (database migration) ./inject-warnings.sh "database migration schema" | prepend-to-task TASK-042
example.note: Keywords match entries by domain, not by full-text similarity. Semantic matching is unreliable in prompts.

section.reference: Bash script for keyword-based warning injection

rule.statement: SDD quality gates L1-L6 are the structural enforcement of lessons from past mistakes. Each gate failure generates a mistake entry.
rule.rationale: Gates create mandatory checkpoints that block progression when known failure conditions are detected, replacing optional checklists.

rule.statement: When a mistake pattern recurs, add an automated CI rule using a file pattern, keyword trigger, or code pattern detector.
rule.rationale: Documentation without enforcement is only awareness. Patterns that survive two documented occurrences require code-level blocking.

example.code: # Prevention rule for destructive migrations (source: MIS-010) id: PREV_010 trigger: type: code_pattern pattern: "DROP (TABLE|COLUMN)" file_types: ["*.sql", "migrations/*"] action: type: block message: "Destructive migration detected. Take backup first. See MIS-010."

rule.statement: For high-severity mistakes (critical/high), add a cross-validation step where a second agent or model reviews the primary output.
rule.rationale: Self-evaluation has a 64.5% blind-spot rate. A second independent pass catches logical inconsistencies the first model cannot see in its own output.
